meta.name: Oualid Bennahia
meta.title: Full Stack Developer
meta.subtitle: PHP & Laravel Specialist
meta.bio: Backend-focused Full Stack Developer specializing in PHP & Laravel with 3+ years of experience building real-world web applications. I deliver complete systems from architecture to deployment.
meta.extended_bio: I hold a Master's degree in Computer Science and have shipped multiple production platforms from scratch. I offer full stack development (Laravel, PHP, JavaScript), REST API design, database optimization (MySQL), AI integration, code review, and mentoring in Arabic and English. Clean code, strong analytics, and transparent collaboration.

sections.contact_subtext: Whether you need a backend system, a full web platform, or an AI-powered application — let's talk.

education.institution: Université Kasdi Merbah de Ouargla, Algeria

projects.title: Competition & Quiz Management System
projects.short_description: Full-stack platform for admins to run multi-level competitions with AI-generated questions, auditor review, and a coin-based economy for competitors.
projects.description: A full-stack competition management platform where admins create and organize competitions across custom topics. Each competition is divided into levels with questions reviewed by auditors or generated via AI. Competitors progress level by level as stages unlock. Users spend coins on AI question generation — earned by winning or purchased. Role management covers admins, auditors, and competitors.

case_study.problem: Educational and corporate organizers needed a single system to run structured quiz competitions with gated levels, quality-controlled questions, and optional AI assistance — without losing oversight from human auditors.
case_study.my_role: Sole full-stack developer: Laravel backend, MySQL schema, REST-style controllers, Blade/Alpine or JS frontends, Tailwind UI, role-based access (admin, auditor, competitor), and integration of an AI question API behind a coin wallet system.
case_study.architecture:
flowchart TB
  subgraph Client
    A[Competitor UI]
    B[Admin / Auditor UI]
  end
  subgraph Laravel
    C[Auth & Roles]
    D[Competition Service]
    E[Question Pipeline]
    F[Coin Wallet]
  end
  subgraph External
    G[(MySQL)]
    H[AI Provider API]
  end
  A --> C
  B --> C
  C --> D
  D --> E
  E --> G
  E --> H
  F --> E
  F --> G
features: Auditor review queue for submitted questions
AI question generation with coin debit

challenges.title: AI cost control
challenges.description: Wrapped AI calls in transactions with coin reservation so failed generations do not drain balances.

challenges.title: Auditor workflow
challenges.description: Designed status transitions (pending → approved/rejected) with audit logs for accountability.

lessons: Domain enums for competition state reduce frontend bugs
Wallet pattern isolates monetization from core quiz logic
Queue workers help for bulk AI generation without blocking HTTP

projects.title: News Web App
projects.short_description: Editorial news platform with admin dashboard, rich article editor, categories/tags, comment moderation, and a public REST API for listings and article pages.
projects.description: A full-stack news management platform with an admin dashboard and public portal. Features include role-based user and permissions management, rich text article editor with cover images and video support, category and tag organization, and comment moderation. Public routes consume a REST API for news listings and article detail pages.

case_study.problem: Editorial teams needed centralized control over articles, media, taxonomy, and reader comments while serving a fast, decoupled public site via API.
case_study.my_role: Built the Laravel admin panel with Spatie-style permissions, WYSIWYG article workflow, media uploads, REST API resources for the public frontend, and Tailwind-styled dashboards.
case_study.architecture:
flowchart LR
  subgraph Admin
    A[Dashboard]
    B[Article Editor]
  end
  subgraph API
    C[Laravel REST]
    D[Auth Sanctum]
  end
  subgraph Public
    E[News SPA / Blade]
  end
  subgraph Data
    F[(MySQL)]
    G[Storage]
  end
  A --> C
  B --> C
  C --> D
  C --> F
  B --> G
  E --> C
features: Rich text editor with cover image and video embeds
REST API for public listings and detail

challenges.title: Media handling
challenges.description: Normalized upload paths and responsive image variants to keep article pages fast.

challenges.title: API consistency
challenges.description: Used API resources and versioning conventions so the public client stays stable as admin features grow.

challenges.title: Comment spam
challenges.description: Added moderation states and rate limiting on public comment endpoints.

lessons: Separate admin and API route files early
Slug history prevents broken links after title edits
Eager-load relationships on list endpoints to avoid N+1

projects.title: Medical Services & Patient Portal
projects.short_description: Full-stack healthcare platform (PHP, HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript) connecting patients with clinics, pharmacies, and labs — appointments, medical files, inquiries, and provider messaging.
projects.description: A full-stack healthcare platform connecting citizens with clinics, pharmacies, and medical laboratories. Patients can search and browse health providers, book appointments online, track their medical files and reservation status, check drug availability at nearby pharmacies, and send inquiries. Health institutions manage their profiles, services, and patient interactions through a dedicated dashboard. Includes reviews, ratings, and real-time messaging between patients and providers.

case_study.problem: Patients lacked a unified way to discover care providers, book visits, follow reservation status, check prescriptions at nearby pharmacies, and contact institutions — while clinics, pharmacies, and labs needed one place to manage profiles and patient interactions.
case_study.my_role: Full-stack web development with PHP on the server side and HTML5, CSS3, and JavaScript on the client: patient and provider portals, appointment booking, medical file tracking, pharmacy availability search, inquiry forms, reviews/ratings, and messaging UI integrated with PHP backends.
case_study.architecture:
flowchart TB
  subgraph Client
    P[Patient Pages HTML/CSS/JS]
    H[Institution Dashboard]
  end
  subgraph Server
    PHP[PHP Application]
    M[Messaging Handlers]
    B[Booking Logic]
  end
  subgraph Data
    DB[(Database)]
  end
  P --> PHP
  H --> PHP
  PHP --> B
  PHP --> M
  PHP --> DB
features: Medical file and reservation status tracking

challenges.title: Multi-institution access
challenges.description: Separated patient, clinic, pharmacy, and lab areas with role-based PHP sessions so each institution only sees its own data.

challenges.title: Booking conflicts
challenges.description: Server-side validation and slot checks in PHP to prevent double bookings.

lessons: Clear PHP include structure keeps patient and admin areas maintainable
Progressive enhancement with vanilla JavaScript avoids heavy frontend dependencies
Real-time messaging UI needs a clear refresh or push strategy from the start
